description_long: This display features two metallic rings, digitally reconstructed to represent coat of mail rings from Xanten, dating from 0-120 AD, as indicated by the museum-style label. The rings are presented on a smooth, glossy blue stand with a vertical back panel that provides historical context and a scale for measurement. One ring is perfectly circular, while the other has a slight deformation, possibly representing wear or a clasp mechanism. The rings have a shiny, golden-bronze appearance, suggesting they are made of metal, likely bronze or a similar alloy. The display is clean and modern, with a checkered scale bar for size reference, emphasizing the historical and archaeological significance of the objects. The overall presentation is minimalistic, focusing attention on the rings themselves.
description: Two metallic, golden-bronze rings are displayed on a glossy blue museum stand with historical labeling and a measurement scale. One ring is circular, the other slightly deformed, both evoking ancient craftsmanship.
description_view_0: Front view shows both rings clearly on the blue stand, with the historical label and measurement scale fully visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thickness and metallic sheen of the rings, with the label partially visible and the rings' profiles emphasized.
description_view_2: Rear view shows only the back of the blue stand, with no rings or label visible.
description_view_3: Angled view provides a sense of depth, showing both rings and the label, with the rings' shapes and the stand's structure visible.
